title: Two-step equations
description: Practice solving equations that take two steps to solve. For example, solve  -16 = x/4 + 2.

hints: Let's subtract and then divide to get $b$ by itself.

$\begin{align} -11b+7 &= 40 \\
\\
-11b+7 \blue{-7}&= 40\blue{-7}~~~~\blue{\text{subtract }7} \text{ from each side}\\
\\
-11b+\cancel{ 7} {\blue{-}\cancel{\blue{7}}}&= 40\blue{-7}\\
\\
-11b &=40\blue{-7}\end{align}$

$\begin{align}-11b &= 33 \\
\\
\dfrac{-11b}{\pink{-11}} &= \dfrac{33}{\pink{-11}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide each side by } \pink{-11} \text{ to get } b \text{ by itself }\\
\\
\dfrac{\cancel{-11}b}{\cancel{\pink{-11}}} &= \dfrac{33}{\pink{-11}} \\
\\
b &= \dfrac{33}{\pink{-11}} \end{align}$

The answer:

$b=\green{-3}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's add and then multiply to get $g$ by itself.

$\begin{align} 3 &=\dfrac g{-4}-5 \\
\\
3\blue{+5} &= \dfrac g{-4}-5\blue{+5}~~~~\blue{\text{add }5} \text{ to each side}\\
\\
3\blue{+5}&=\dfrac g{-4}-\cancel{ 5} {\blue{+}\cancel{\blue{5}}}\\
\\
3\blue{+5}&=\dfrac g{-4}\end{align}$

$\begin{align}8 &= \dfrac g{-4} \\
\\
{8}\cdot{\pink{-4}} &= \dfrac{g}{-4}\cdot{\pink{-4}} ~~~~~~~\text{multiply each side by } \pink{-4} \text{ to get } g \text{ by itself }\\
\\
 8\cdot{\pink{-4}}&=\dfrac g{\cancel{-4}}\cdot{\cancel{\pink{-4}}}  \\
\\ {8}\cdot{\pink{-4}}&=g \end{align}$

The answer:

$g=\green{-32}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's add and then multiply to get $h$ by itself.

$\begin{align} \dfrac{h}6-1 &= -3\\
\\
 \dfrac{h}6-1 \blueD{+1}&= -3\blueD{+1}~~~~\blueD{\text{add }1} \text{ to each side}\\
\\
\dfrac h6-\cancel{ 1} {\blueD{+}\cancel{\blueD{1}}}&= -3\blueD{+1}\\
\\
\dfrac h6 &=-3\blueD{+1}\end{align}$

$\begin{align}\dfrac h6 &= -2 \\
\\
\dfrac{h}6\cdot{\goldD{6}} &= {-2}\cdot{\goldD{6}} ~~~~~~~\text{multiply each side by } \goldD{6} \\
\\
\dfrac h{\cancel{6}}\cdot{\cancel{\goldD{6}}} &= -2\cdot{\goldD{6}} \\
\\
h &= -2\cdot{\goldD{6}} \end{align}$

The answer:

$h=\greenD{-12}$

Let's check our work:

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{h}6-1 &= -3\\\\
 \dfrac{\greenD{-12}}6-1 &\stackrel{?}{=} -3 \\\\
 -2-1 &\stackrel{?}{=} -3 \\\\
-3 &= -3 ~~~~~~~~~~~~\text{Yes!}
\end{align}$
Let's subtract and then multiply to get $j$ by itself.

$\begin{align} \dfrac{j}{-2}+ 7&= -12\\
\\
 \dfrac{j}{-2}+7 \blue{-7}&= -12\blue{-7}~~~~\blue{\text{subtract }7} \text{ from each side}\\
\\
\dfrac j{-2}+\cancel{ 7} {\blue{-}\cancel{\blue{7}}}&= -12\blue{-7}\\
\\
\dfrac j{-2} &=-12\blue{-7}\end{align}$

$\begin{align}\dfrac j{-2} &= -19 \\
\\
\dfrac{j}{-2}\cdot{\pink{-2}} &= {-19}\cdot{\pink{-2}} ~~~~~~~\text{multiply each side by } \pink{-2} \text{ to get } j \text{ by itself }\\
\\
\dfrac j{\cancel{-2}}\cdot{\cancel{\pink{-2}}} &= -19\cdot{\pink{-2}} \\
\\
j &=-19\cdot{\pink{-2}} \end{align}$

The answer:

$j=\green{38}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's subtract and then multiply to get $r$ by itself.

$\begin{align} -13 &=\dfrac r9+8 \\
\\
-13\blue{-8} &= \dfrac r9+8\blue{-8}~~~~~\blue{\text{subtract }8} \text{ from each side}\\
\\
-13\blue{-8}&=\dfrac r9+\cancel{ 8} {\blue{-}\cancel{\blue{8}}}\\
\\
-13\blue{-8}&=\dfrac r9\end{align}$

$\begin{align}-21 &= \dfrac r9 \\
\\
{-21}\cdot{\pink{9}} &= \dfrac{r}9\cdot{\pink{9}} ~~~~~~~\text{multiply each side by } \pink{9} \text{ to get } r \text{ by itself }\\
\\
 -21\cdot{\pink{9}}&=\dfrac r{\cancel{9}}\cdot{\cancel{\pink{9}}}  \\
\\ {-21}\cdot{\pink{9}}&=r \end{align}$

The answer:

$r=\green{-189}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's subtract and then divide to get $m$ by itself.

$\begin{align} 13 &=2m+5 \\
\\
13\blue{-5} &= 2m+5\blue{-5}~~~~\blue{\text{subtract }5} \text{ from each side}\\
\\
13\blue{-5}&=2m+\cancel{ 5} {\blue{-}\cancel{\blue{5}}}\\
\\
13\blue{-5}&=2m\end{align}$

$\begin{align}8 &= 2m \\
\\
\dfrac{8}{\pink{2}} &= \dfrac{2m}{\pink{2}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide each side by } \pink{2} \text{ to get } m \text{ by itself }\\
\\
 \dfrac8{\pink{2}}&=\dfrac{\cancel{2}m}{\cancel{\pink{2}}}  \\
\\ \dfrac{8}{\pink{2}}&=m \end{align}$

The answer:

$m=\green{4}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's add and then divide to get $d$ by itself.

$\begin{align} 41 &=12d-7 \\
\\
41\blue{+7} &= 12d-7\blue{+7}~~~~~~\blue{\text{add }7} \text{ to each side}\\
\\
41\blue{+7}&=12d-\cancel{ 7} {\blue{+}\cancel{\blue{7}}}\\
\\
41\blue{+7}&=12d\end{align}$

$\begin{align}48 &= 12d \\
\\
\dfrac{48}{\pink{12}} &= \dfrac{12d}{\pink{12}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide each side by } \pink{12} \text{ to get } d \text{ by itself }\\
\\
 \dfrac{48}{\pink{12}}&=\dfrac{\cancel{12}d}{\cancel{\pink{12}}}  \\
\\ \dfrac{48}{\pink{12}}&=d \end{align}$

The answer:

$d=\green{4}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's add and then divide to get $n$ by itself.

$\begin{align} 7n-4 &= 31 \\
\\
7n-4 \blue{+4}&= 31\blue{+4}~~~~\blue{\text{add }4} \text{ to each side}\\
\\
7n-\cancel{ 4} {\blue{+}\cancel{\blue{4}}}&= 31\blue{+4}\\
\\
7n &=31\blue{+4}\end{align}$

$\begin{align}7n &= 35 \\
\\
\dfrac{7n}{\pink{7}} &= \dfrac{35}{\pink{7}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide each side by } \pink{7} \text{ to get } n \text{ by itself }\\
\\
\dfrac{\cancel{7}n}{\cancel{\pink{7}}} &= \dfrac{35}{\pink{7}} \\
\\
n &= \dfrac{35}{\pink{7}} \end{align}$

The answer:

$n=\green{5}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's add and then multiply to get $m$ by itself.

$\begin{align} 2& =\dfrac m2-7 \\
\\
2\blue{+7} &= \dfrac m2-7\blue{+7}~~~~\blue{\text{add }7} \text{ to each side}\\
\\
2\blue{+7}&=\dfrac m2-\cancel{ 7} {\blue{+}\cancel{\blue{7}}}\\
\\
2\blue{+7}&=\dfrac m2\end{align}$

$\begin{align}9 &= \dfrac m2 \\
\\
{9}\cdot{\pink{2}} &= \dfrac m{2}\cdot{\pink{2}} ~~~~~~~\text{multiply each side by } \pink{2} \text{ to get } m \text{ by itself }\\
\\
 9\cdot{\pink{2}}&=\dfrac m{\cancel{2}}\cdot{\cancel{\pink{2}}}  \\
\\ {9}\cdot{\pink{2}}&=m \end{align}$

The answer:

$m=\green{18}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's subtract and then multiply to get $k$ by itself.

$\begin{align} \dfrac{k}4+ 3&= 14\\
\\
 \dfrac{k}4+3 \blue{-3}&= 14\blue{-3}~~~~\blue{\text{subtract }3} \text{ from each side}\\
\\
\dfrac k4+\cancel{ 3} {\blue{-}\cancel{\blue{3}}}&= 14\blue{-3}\\
\\
\dfrac k4&=14\blue{-3}\end{align}$

$\begin{align}\dfrac k4 &= 11 \\
\\
\dfrac{k}4\cdot{\pink{4}} &= {11}\cdot{\pink{4}} ~~~~~~~\text{multiply each side by } \pink{4} \text{ to get } k \text{ by itself }\\
\\
\dfrac k{\cancel{4}}\cdot{\cancel{\pink{4}}} &= 11\cdot{\pink{4}} \\
\\
k &= 11\cdot{\pink{4}} \end{align}$

The answer:

$k=\green{44}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's divide and then add to get $z$ by itself.

$\begin{align}42&=-7(z-3) \\
\\\\
\dfrac{42}{\pink{-7}} &= \dfrac{-7(z-3)}{\pink{-7}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide each side by } \pink{-7}\\
\\
\dfrac{42}{\pink{-7}} &= \dfrac{\cancel{7}(z-3)}{\cancel{{\pink{-7}}}} \\
\\
\dfrac{42}{\pink{-7}}&= z-3\end{align}$

$\begin{align} -6&= z-3 \\
\\
-6 \blue{+3}&= z-3\blue{+3}~~~~~~~\blue{\text{add }3}  \text{ to each side to get } z \text{ by itself }\\
\\
-6\blue{+3}&= z-\cancel{3}\blue{+\cancel{3}}\\
\\
-6\blue{+3} &=z\end{align}$

The answer:

$z=\green{-3}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's divide and then add to get $p$ by itself.

$\begin{align}9(p-4)&=-18 \\
\\\\
\dfrac{9(p-4)}{\pink{9}} &= \dfrac{-18}{\pink{9}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide each side by } \pink{9}\\
\\
\dfrac{\cancel{9}(p-4)}{\cancel{\pink9}} &= \dfrac{-18}{{\pink{9}}} \\
\\
 p-4&=\dfrac{-18}{\pink9}\end{align}$

$\begin{align} p-4&= -2 \\
\\
p-4 \blue{+4}&= -2\blue{+4}~~~~~~~\blue{\text{add }4}  \text{ to each side to get } p \text{ by itself }\\
\\
p-\cancel{4}\blue{+\cancel{4}}&=-2\blue{+4} \\
\\
p&=-2\blue{+4} \end{align}$

The answer:

$p=\green{2}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's divide and then subtract to get $x$ by itself.

$\begin{align}-30&=5(x+1) \\
\\\\
\dfrac{-30}{\pink{5}} &= \dfrac{5(x+1)}{\pink{5}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide each side by } \pink{5}\\
\\
\dfrac{-30}{\pink5} &= \dfrac{\cancel{5}(x+1)}{\cancel{{\pink{5}}}} \\
\\
\dfrac{-30}{\pink5}&= x+1\end{align}$

$\begin{align} -6&= x+1 \\
\\
-6 \blue{-1}&= x+1\blue{-1}~~~~\blue{\text{subtract }1}  \text{ to get } x \text{ by itself }\\
\\
-6\blue{-1}&= x+\cancel{1}\blue{-\cancel{1}}\\
\\
-6\blue{-1} &=x\end{align}$

The answer:

$x=\green{-7}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's divide and then subtract to get $w$ by itself.

$\begin{align}-4(w+1) &= -24 \\
\\
\dfrac{-4(w+1)}{\pink{-4}} &= \dfrac{-24}{\pink{-4}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide each side by } \pink{-4}\\
\\
\dfrac{\cancel{-4}(w+1)}{\cancel{\pink{-4}}} &= \dfrac{-24}{\pink{-4}} \\
\\
w+1 &= \dfrac{-24}{\pink{-4}} \end{align}$

$\begin{align} w +1&= 6 \\
\\
w+1 \blue{-1}&= 6\blue{-1}~~~~\blue{\text{subtract }1} \text{ from each side} \text{ to get } w \text{ by itself }\\
\\
w+\cancel{ 1} {\blue{-}\cancel{\blue{1}}}&= 6\blue{-1}\\
\\
w &=6\blue{-1}\end{align}$

The answer:

$w=\green{5}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's divide and then add to get $n$ by itself.

$\begin{align}24&=3(n-5) \\
\\\\
\dfrac{24}{\pink{3}} &= \dfrac{3(n-5)}{\pink{3}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide each side by } \pink{3}\\
\\
\dfrac{24}{\pink3} &= \dfrac{\cancel{3}(n-5)}{\cancel{{\pink{3}}}} \\
\\
\dfrac{24}{\pink3}&= n-5\end{align}$

$\begin{align} 8&= n-5 \\
\\
8 \blue{+5}&= n-5\blue{+5}~~~~~~~\blue{\text{add }5}  \text{ to each side to get } n \text{ by itself }\\
\\
8\blue{+5}&= n-\cancel{5}\blue{+\cancel{5}}\\
\\
8\blue{+5} &=n\end{align}$

The answer:

$n=\green{13}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's divide and then add to get $q$ by itself.

$\begin{align}3(q-7)&=27 \\
\\\\
\dfrac{3(q-7)}{\pink{3}} &= \dfrac{27}{\pink{3}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide each side by } \pink{3}\\
\\
\dfrac{\cancel{3}(q-7)}{\cancel{\pink3}} &= \dfrac{27}{{\pink{3}}} \\
\\
 q-7&=\dfrac{27}{\pink3}\end{align}$

$\begin{align} q-7&= 9 \\
\\
q-7 \blue{+7}&= 9\blue{+7}~~~~~~~\blue{\text{add }7}  \text{ to each side to get } q \text{ by itself }\\
\\
q-\cancel{7}\blue{+\cancel{7}}&=9\blue{+7} \\
\\
q&=9\blue{+7} \end{align}$

The answer:

$q=\green{16}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's divide and then subtract to get $t$ by itself.

$\begin{align}2(t+1)& = 10 \\
\\
\dfrac{2(t+1)}{\pink{2}} &= \dfrac{10}{\pink{2}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide each side by } \pink{2}\\
\\
\dfrac{\cancel{2}(t+1)}{\cancel{\pink{2}}} &= \dfrac{10}{\pink{2}} \\
\\
t+1 &= \dfrac{10}{\pink{2}} \end{align}$

$\begin{align} t +1&= 5 \\
\\
t+1 \blue{-1}&= 5\blue{-1}~~~~\blue{\text{subtract }1} \text{ from each side} \text{ to get } t \text{ by itself }\\
\\
t+\cancel{ 1} {\blue{-}\cancel{\blue{1}}}&= 5\blue{-1}\\
\\
t &=5\blue{-1}\end{align}$

The answer:

$t=\green{4}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
Let's divide and then subtract to get $y$ by itself.

$\begin{align}6&=2(y+2) \\
\\\\
\dfrac{6}{\pink{2}} &= \dfrac{2(y+2)}{\pink{2}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide each side by } \pink{2}\\
\\
\dfrac{6}{\pink2} &= \dfrac{\cancel{2}(y+2)}{\cancel{{\pink{2}}}} \\
\\
\dfrac{6}{\pink2}&= y+2\end{align}$

$\begin{align} 3&= y+2 \\
\\
3 \blue{-2}&= y+2\blue{-2}~~~~\blue{\text{subtract }2}  \text{ to get } y \text{ by itself }\\
\\
3\blue{-2}&= y+\cancel{2}\blue{-\cancel{2}}\\
\\
3\blue{-2} &=y\end{align}$

The answer:

$y=\green{1}~~~~~~~~$[[☃ explanation 1]]
